examples.title: Calculate Network Address
examples.description: Algorithm to find network address from IP and subnet mask
examples.explanation: Network address is obtained by performing bitwise AND between IP address and subnet mask. All host bits become 0.

examples.title: Calculate Usable Hosts
examples.description: Calculate number of usable hosts in a given CIDR prefix
examples.code:
FUNCTION CalculateUsableHosts(cidrPrefix)
 // Total bits in IPv4 = 32
 totalBits = 32
 
 // Host bits = total bits - network bits
 hostBits = totalBits - cidrPrefix
 
 // Total addresses = 2^hostBits
 totalAddresses = 2 ^ hostBits
 
 // Usable hosts = total - network address - broadcast
 usableHosts = totalAddresses - 2
 
 // Handle edge case for /31 and /32
 IF cidrPrefix == 31 THEN
 usableHosts = 2 // Point-to-point links
 ELSE IF cidrPrefix == 32 THEN
 usableHosts = 1 // Single host
 END IF
 
 RETURN usableHosts
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: For most subnets, usable hosts = 2^(32-CIDR) - 2. Special cases exist for /31 and /32 point-to-point links.

examples.title: Binary to Decimal Conversion
examples.code:
FUNCTION BinaryToDecimal(binaryString)
 decimal = 0
 length = LENGTH(binaryString)
 
 FOR i FROM 0 TO length-1
 // Get bit from left to right
 bit = binaryString[i]
 
 // Calculate position from right (7-i for MSB first)
 position = length - 1 - i
 
 // Add to decimal if bit is 1
 IF bit == '1' THEN
 decimal = decimal + (2 ^ position)
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 RETURN decimal
END FUNCTION

// Example: BinaryToDecimal("11001010") returns 202
// 128 + 64 + 8 + 2 = 202
examples.explanation: Each position in an 8-bit binary number represents powers of 2 (128,64,32,16,8,4,2,1). Add values where bit is 1.
